summary.en: A unique blend of breathtaking alpine scenery and a well-preserved Old West frontier spirit, this entire district is a National Historic landmark nestled in a dramatic box canyon.

description.en: A meticulously recreated 19th-century apothecary that feels more like a museum, filled with curious tinctures, historical artifacts, and strange medical devices.
A natural wonder featuring a powerful waterfall in a narrow slot canyon, accessible via a suspended steel catwalk just above the roaring water.
A historic 1888 courthouse famous for its appearance in the classic John Wayne film 'True Grit', offering a tangible link to cinematic history.
Home to Ouray's tallest and most visible waterfall, this park offers a stunning scenic payoff via a short, accessible trail to the base of the lower falls.

prompt.en: Find a view that looks like a scene from a classic Western movie and capture it. Bonus points for dramatic angles!
Take a selfie that perfectly captures the 'Switzerland of America' vibe, with majestic peaks in the background.
Zoom in on an interesting architectural detail on one of the Victorian-era buildings. It could be ornate trim, a unique window, or a colorful facade.
Frame a photo that contrasts the historic, human-scale buildings of Main Street with the immense, sheer mountain peaks towering above them.
Find a sign, object, or building that you feel best represents Ouray's rugged, independent frontier spirit.
Capture the reflection of the dramatic canyon walls or a historic building in a shop window.
Find a menu posted outside a restaurant or cafe. Snap a photo of the most interesting or mountain-themed item on it.
Pose for a photo in front of a historic building as if you've just arrived from the 1880s.
Close your eyes and listen for a moment. What natural sound can you hear from downtown? The wind, a creek, a waterfall? Take a photo of what you think is making the sound.
Ouray inspired a mythical place in 'Atlas Shrugged'. Capture a photo of a scene that looks rugged, hidden, and self-sufficient.
The Old West wasn't all brown and grey. Find the most colorfully painted historic building and take a picture.
Find a historical plaque or marker on a building. Take a photo and share one interesting fact you learned from it.
Peer into a cafe or restaurant and find the coziest-looking spot to enjoy a warm drink. Snap a photo of it from the outside.
Use a vertical panorama to try and capture the full height of the scene, from the street at your feet to the peak of the mountains above.
Play with the light and shadows cast by the tall canyon walls. Capture an interesting pattern of light on a building or street.
Find an example of the new and old side-by-side, like a modern car parked in front of a 140-year-old building, and capture the contrast.
Take a selfie that says 'I'm an adventurer exploring a historic mountain town'.
Find a business sign with a classic, old-fashioned font or design and take a photo of it.
